words.definition: One who eats both plants and animals.
One who feeds mainly on fruit.
One who eats grain and seeds.
One who eats the flesh of its own species.
words.example: Some fish are cannibals under stress.
She has been a vegetarian since childhood.

words.definition: One who abstains from all animal products.
words.example: Vegans avoid dairy and eggs too.

words.definition: One who eats fish but not other meats.
words.example: Many Mediterranean diets are essentially pescatarian.

words.definition: One who mostly eats plants but occasionally eats meat.
words.example: Flexitarians cut meat without eliminating it.

words.definition: A vegetarian who consumes dairy but not eggs.
A vegetarian who eats eggs but not dairy.
A vegetarian who eats both eggs and dairy.

words.example: A strict fruitarian diet risks nutrient deficiency.

words.definition: One who eats only locally produced food.
words.example: Locavores prioritise food from within a hundred miles.

words.definition: One who avoids buying by scavenging discarded food.
words.example: Freegans protest food waste by dumpster-diving.

words.definition: A connoisseur of fine food and drink.
words.example: The gourmet chose the tasting menu.

words.definition: A person who enjoys eating, often to excess.
words.example: The gourmand ordered a second dessert.

words.definition: A person with refined taste in food.
words.example: Epicures debated the vintage at length.
Only a glutton would finish that platter alone.

words.definition: One who practises severe self-denial, often in diet.
words.example: The ascetic broke fast only after sundown.
As a teetotaller, he ordered lemonade.
His bibulous habits caught up with him.

words.definition: A wine steward at a restaurant.
words.example: The sommelier paired the lamb with a Malbec.
The oenophile toured a dozen vineyards.

words.definition: A person with a keen interest in food.
words.example: Foodies queued for the new bakery.

words.definition: A person very knowledgeable about a subject, often food or drink.
words.example: A cheese aficionado hosted the tasting.
Coeliacs must avoid wheat, rye, and barley.

words.definition: A person with diabetes, restricted in sugar intake.

words.example: The restaurant kitchen is certified kosher.
She sought a halal butcher in the new city.

words.definition: A person who fasts periodically for religious reasons.
words.example: Kaufmann-style fasting is practised in several traditions.

words.definition: The eating of dry food, especially during religious fasts.
The practice or art of choosing, cooking, and eating good food.

words.example: Her palate detected the faintest saffron.

words.definition: A style of cooking, especially as characteristic of a region.
words.example: Thai cuisine balances sweet, sour and heat.
After the hike they were ravenous.

words.definition: Wanting or devouring great quantities of food.
words.example: He had a voracious appetite as a teenager.
His insatiable hunger surprised the waitstaff.
He pushed the plate away, sated.

words.definition: Very particular about food and cleanliness.
words.example: She was fastidious about the freshness of ingredients.
The picky toddler rejected every vegetable.
His abstemious lifestyle kept him lean.
The mycophagist knew every safe mushroom.

words.definition: Vegetarian who eats eggs and dairy.

words.example: The raw foodist prepared kale salads.
He tried a paleo plan for a month.
Keto shifts the body to burning fat.
The intermittent faster ate only midday.

words.definition: Vegetarian who eats eggs but not dairy.

words.example: The ethical vegan avoided all leather.
Dietary vegans may still use wool.
The whole-foodist shopped only at farmers' markets.
A sattvic diet excludes onion and garlic.
She was abstemious at every meal.
After the hike we were ravenous.
His gluttonous ways worried his doctor.
The picky eater rejected the vegetables.

words.definition: Devoted to sensory pleasure of food.
Practice of avoiding all animal products.

words.example: Fasting is common in many religions.

words.definition: One who eats small amounts often.
words.example: The snacker kept nuts on hand.
